entry.title: UN03 — Soundscape for Performance on Presence and Neglect

link.title: UN03 — Soundscape for Performance on Presence and Neglect

entry.content:
In August 2025 I worked with dancer and performer Awa Jalali on UN03, presented at dbstudio in Berlin.

I produced the soundscape for the piece.

Structure

The work combined two main elements:

  • an opening sound collage built from found aircraft recordings
  • a narrated voice track processed through stretching, layering, and timing shifts

The main challenge was keeping the voice present while letting it drift in and out of clarity.

The final soundscape ran for roughly 20 minutes.
entry.summary: Soundscape work for Awa Jalali’s performance UN03 at dbstudio in Berlin.

entry.title: The Elephant in the Room – VCV Rack Patch for InKüle Performance

link.title: The Elephant in the Room – VCV Rack Patch for InKüle Performance

entry.content:
The Elephant in the Room is a VCV Rack patch prepared for a 20-minute InKüLe performance shown as part of the UdK 50-year anniversary program on February 8, 2025.

Patch setup

The patch generates ambient textures, chord swarms, and rhythmic layers. I use a Korg NanoKontrol2 for live control:

  • Mixer channels
    1. Drone
    2. Chord swarm
    3. Sequence pluck
    4. Drum part
    5. High texture layer
  • Rotary knobs
    • Bernoulli gate probability
    • Delay modulation
    • Basic filter shaping

Patch link: The Elephant in the Room on Patchstorage

Demonstration Video

This page is mainly documentation for the patch and demo video.
entry.summary: The Elephant in the Room is a VCV Rack patch prepared for a 20-minute InKüLe performance shown as part of the UdK 50-year anniversary program on February 8, 2025.

entry.title: Check out my patches
entry.content:
This page links to my Patchstorage profile, where I upload VCV Rack patches and small experiments.

Most of them are built around generative structures, controller mapping, or live performance use.

  • Patchstorage profile

If you use one of the patches and need context, feel free to get in touch.
entry.summary: This page links to my Patchstorage profile, where I upload VCV Rack patches and small experiments.

entry.title: MOOC on Sound
entry.content:
This post is a reference page for the Experiment with Sounds MOOC.

It is not meant as a full essay or course summary. I use it mainly as a compact public note listing some of the artists, researchers, and topics that came up during the course.

Topics

  • sound studies
  • listening and silence
  • improvisation
  • field recording
  • sound and architecture
  • rhythm, timbre, and duration
  • streaming and studio practice

People and references

  • Charles Gaines
  • Anna Halprin
  • Beau Dick
  • Brian Eno
  • Scott Lindroth
  • Christina Kubisch
  • Alvin Lucier
  • Bernhard Leitner
  • Thomas Kusitzky
  • Christopher DeLaurenti

If needed, I can later turn this into a more useful resources page with links, reading notes, and selected takeaways instead of a raw course record.
entry.summary: This post is a reference page for the Experiment with Sounds MOOC.

entry.title: Celestial Evidence
entry.content:
Setup

Celestial Evidence is a hybrid performance built with VCV Rack, electric guitar, and post-mixed audio.

The patch is built around a C# minor loop and four main layers:

  • A quantized sequence
  • A reversed tape-based layer of the same sequence
  • An FM bass voice
  • A drum section built from kick, noise, and tape delay

Because of CPU limits, the guitar was recorded separately as a DI track in Reaper and then mixed with the patch output afterwards.

Video

Patch download:

👉 Celestial Evidence on Patchstorage
entry.summary: A hybrid modular-guitar performance built with VCV Rack, electric guitar, and post-mixed audio.

entry.title: Violet Bubbles – A December 2022 Snapshot
entry.content:
Violet Bubbles is a VCV Rack patch and one-take performance from December 2022.

The Patch

I built this patch in VCV Rack (free version). It is structured around:

  • A quantized sequence running through two oscillators
  • A simple drum machine with delayed reflections
  • A live-played bass part on a MIDI controller
  • Delayed and modulated layers added over time
  • A form that begins and ends in noise

Patch download: https://patchstorage.com/violet-bubbles/

A One-Take Performance

This recording was made quickly and left mostly as it was:

  • I decided to film it late in the process and recorded it in one take.
  • The guitar and vocal part was mixed in during video editing.

This post is mainly here as documentation of the patch and the recording.
entry.summary: Violet Bubbles is a VCV Rack patch and one-take performance from December 2022.

entry.title: Midnight Euphoria
entry.content:
Setup

Midnight Euphoria is a home-studio performance built around a VCV Rack patch, guitar, and live controller mapping.

The patch was designed to handle drums, arpeggios, and pads while I played over it live.

Main elements:

  • A drum machine for pulse
  • Arpeggiators and sequenced material
  • Ambient pad layers
  • Guitar and occasional synth on top
  • MIDI control for switching and shaping the patch

Patch: Midnight Euphoria on Patchstorage

Audio

Eman Safavi · Midnight Euphoria

Video

This page is mainly here as documentation of the patch and the performance.
entry.summary: A home-studio performance built with VCV Rack, guitar, and live controller mapping.
